title: Weather & Seasons: Learn English & Spanish
description: A vocabulary pack designed for K-2 ELL newcomers to learn basic weather conditions and seasons in English and Spanish, featuring simple words, visual aids, and engaging activities.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A bright yellow smiling sun in a blue sky.
Dark clouds with raindrops falling, a child holding an umbrella.
Falling snowflakes, a snowman with a hat and scarf.
A big fluffy white cloud floating in the sky.
A tree bending in the wind, leaves blowing, a kite flying.
A sun shining brightly, a child sweating and fanning themselves.
A child bundled in a hat and scarf, shivering slightly, with snow in the background.
Green grass, blooming flowers (tulips, daffodils), baby animals (chick, bunny).
A beach scene with a bright sun, ocean, sandcastle, and children playing.
Trees with red, orange, and yellow leaves, leaves falling to the ground, a pumpkin.
Snowy landscape, bare trees, children playing in snow, wearing warm clothes.
A colorful rainbow arching across a sky with light clouds and sunshine.

worksheets.instructions_en: Match the English word to the correct picture. Draw a line from the word to the image.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Une la palabra en inglés con el dibujo correcto. Dibuja una línea desde la palabra hasta la imagen.

items.image_prompt: A sun shining brightly, child sweating
A child bundled in a hat and scarf
A colorful rainbow arching across a sky

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the weather and season words.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras de clima y estaciones.

items.image_prompt: A small sun with sunglasses icon

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the pictures and trace the words for each season.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea los dibujos y traza las palabras para cada estación.

items.image_prompt: A scene for Spring with blooming flowers and a butterfly.
A scene for Summer with children playing at the beach.
A scene for Fall with trees showing colorful leaves and a pumpkin.
A scene for Winter with snow falling and a snowman.

mini_book.title: The Weather and Seasons / El Clima y las Estaciones

pages.text_en: The sun shines bright. It is hot!
Sometimes it rains. We need an umbrella.
pages.text_l2: A veces llueve. Necesitamos un paraguas.
pages.image_prompt: Rain falling from a cloud onto an open umbrella.

pages.text_en: In winter, it can snow. It is cold!
pages.image_prompt: A child wearing a hat and scarf, with snowflakes around them.

pages.text_en: Clouds are in the sky. Wind blows the leaves.
pages.text_l2: Hay nubes en el cielo. El viento sopla las hojas.
pages.image_prompt: Fluffy clouds in the sky and leaves blowing from a tree.

pages.text_en: Spring brings flowers. Summer is for playing outside.
pages.text_l2: La primavera trae flores. El verano es para jugar afuera.
pages.image_prompt: A field of colorful flowers for Spring, and a child playing with a ball on a sunny day for Summer.

pages.text_en: Fall has colorful leaves. Winter has snow and ice.
pages.text_l2: El otoño tiene hojas de colores. El invierno tiene nieve y hielo.
pages.image_prompt: Red and orange leaves falling for Fall, and a snowy landscape with a snowflake for Winter.

pages.text_en: What is your favorite weather or season?
pages.text_l2: ¿Cuál es tu clima o estación favorita?
pages.image_prompt: A child thinking, with thought bubbles showing various weather and season icons.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and name common weather conditions and seasons in English and Spanish, using age-appropriate vocabulary and visual cues.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary with flashcards and gestures. Read the mini-book aloud, pointing to pictures. Use worksheets for guided practice and independent reinforcement. Encourage daily 'weather talk' in class. The mini-book can be folded and stapled by students.
differentiation_tips: Support: Pre-teach vocabulary with realia or gestures. Pair students with a stronger English speaker. Allow pointing as an answer. Provide sentence frames for verbal responses (e.g., 'It is ___').

Challenge: Encourage students to use the vocabulary in simple sentences (e.g., 'I like the sun because it is hot.'). Ask them to describe what they wear or do in different weather/seasons. Have them draw and label their own weather scenes.

answers.4: A sun shining brightly, child sweating
answers.5: A child bundled in a hat and scarf
answers.6: A colorful rainbow arching across a sky

answer_key.answers: Tracing words are self-explanatory; students should trace the provided English words.
Coloring activity with tracing. Students should color the images for Spring, Summer, Fall, and Winter, and trace the corresponding English words.
